title: General HuProt microarray protocol [Nicole Lee]
steps: Prepare the following buffers and ensure enough of each reagent is available for the HuProt protocol: Detection Buffer: 5% BSA in 1X TBS-T, filtered using a 0.45 um filtration system, store at 4°C until use Bovine serum albumin (IgG-Free, Protease-Free): Jackson ImmunoResearch Laboratories 001-000-162 2 . 1x TBS-T Buffer (also used as Washing Buffer) Tris-buffered saline (pH 7.4), 0.1% Tween-20. Store the buffer at room temperature 3. ddH2O 4. Secondary antibodies
Thaw the frozen sample (serum, plasma, or cerebrospinal fluid) and place on ice.

Place tubes of CDIArrayBlock and Seromics Sample Buffer from storage at 4°C and place on ice (one tube of each buffer is needed for each HuProt microarray unless specific protocol indicates differing quantities, for example CSF).
Add serum, plasma, or CSF to the Seromics Sample Buffer and invert to mix.

Incubate at room temperature for 1 hour with gentle shaking or inversion. This incubation step can be done when the HuProt microarrays are being blocked (see next HuProt microarray preparation section).
Add 3.0 mL of CDIArrayBlock buffer to a clean well plate (NUNC, Thermo Scientific) for each sample.

After all wells are filled with CDIArrayBlock buffer, remove plastic slide holders containing the HuProt arrays from the -80° C freezer, and place the holders flat on a layer of dry ice. The barcodes on the microarrays should be facing up.

Carefully remove one HuProt microarray from a plastic slide holder with fine-nosed tweezers, holding on to the side with the barcode. NOTE: The active surface of the HuProt microarray is also the barcoded side. The microarrays should be handled only at the barcoded end. Wear gloves and use tweezers to handle the microarrays.
Immediately, submerge the microarray in one blotting box with the CDIArrayBlock buffer, keeping the active/barcoded side of the microarray facing up.

When all microarrays have been added to their respective blotting boxes, gently move the box in a circular fashion until the blocking solution covers the entire surface of each array. Ensure full coverage.
Incubate the arrays at room temperature for 1 hour with gentle shaking (20-40 rpm).
Carefully decant the liquid from each blotting box (the HuProt microarray will stick to the bottom of the blotting box).

Carefully add the incubated prepared sample to the microarray, adding slowly from the barcoded area of the blocked HuProt microarray. Do not touch the microarray surface with pipette tip/tube. Ensure full coverage of microarray.
Incubate the slides on an orbital shaker (20-40 rpm) at room temperature for 1 hour.
Following incubation, carefully decant liquid from each blotting box.
Rinse (1x): slowly add 4.0 mL of 1x TBS-T solution to each blotting box from the barcoded end of the microarray. Quickly rinse the microarray for 5 seconds, then decant the liquid.

Washing (3x): Slowly refill each well with 4.0mL of 1x TBS-T solution from the barcoded end of the slide. Incubate on an orbital shaker (20-40 rpm) for 10 minutes at room temperature. Decant the liquid.
Repeat for a total of three 10 minute washes in 1x TBS-T.
Dilute the secondary antibody in Detection Buffer (5% BSA) immediately before use. Protect from light using aluminum foil. Refer to specific protocol for calculated volumes.
Decant the liquid covering the washed HuProt microarrays from blotting boxes. I
Add 3.0 mL of prepared secondary antibody solution to each HuProt microarray. Protect the HuProt slides from light using aluminum foil.
Incubate HuProt slides on orbital shaker (20-30 rpm) for 1 hour at room temperature, ensuring each slide is protected from light.
Carefully decant the buffer containing the secondary antibody from the blotting boxes.
Rinse (1x): Slowly add 4.0 mL 1x TBS-T over barcoded end of the microarray. Quickly rinse for 5 seconds and decant liquid.

Wash (3x): Slowly add 4.0 mL 1x TBS-T over barcoded end of the microarray and incubate for 10 minutes at room temperature on orbital shaker (20-30 rpm). Ensure slides continue to be protected from light.
Repeat for a total of three 10 minute washes in 1x TBS-T.
ddH2O Rinse: Carefully add 4.0 mL of ddH2O to the barcoded end of each microarray. Rinse for 5 seconds. Then carefully decant the ddH2O.
Repeat for a total of three rinses in ddH2O.
Leave the microarray in ddH2O after the third rinse and immediately proceed to the drying step.
Carefully use tweezers to remove one HuProt microarray (submerged in ddH2O). Only handle the microarray from the barcode. Do not touch the active area of the HuProt microarray at any point.
Gently tap a corner of the microarray onto a clean paper towel to wick away excess water.

Attach a 1000 uL pipette tip to tubing/a hose, and connect the tube to a pressurized air source. Very gently, turn on the air until a consistent stream of air comes from the pipette tip. NOTE: Pressurized air can be dangerous. Wear any appropriate PPE to protect your eyes, and keep the pipette tip/air away from your body and others.

Use the air to dry the microarray until fully dried, blowing away from the barcoded area. Dry the front and back of the microarray. Store the microarray in a microscope box and protect from light using aluminium foil.
The HuProt microarray should be scanned immediately after drying (highly preferable), or stored at 4°C in a lightproof microscope box and scan the microarrays the next day.
Scan on Genepix 4000B. Store HuProt microarrays in a lightproof box at 4°C.
creator: Nicole Lee
